text: Achilles tendinopathy is a painful overuse injury that is extremely common in athletes participating in running and jumping sports.
population: athletes in running and jumping sports

text: Achilles tendinopathy causes alterations in tendon structure and mechanical properties, altered lower extremity function, and fear of movement, all of which limit sport participation and performance.
Progressive tendon loading should be the centerpiece of a comprehensive treatment plan for Achilles tendinopathy to ensure full recovery and minimize reinjury risk.
Full recovery from Achilles tendinopathy requires a thorough evaluation and structured return-to-sport planning, not just pain resolution.
Treatment of Achilles tendinopathy should address not only pain but also tendon health, lower extremity function, and psychological factors such as fear of movement.
